channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Understanding Why Tuberculosis Still Kills Many Nigerians Despite Free Treatment
item.description: Even with medical progress and free TB treatment available across Nigeria, plenty people still dey die from the disease every year. The main wahala na late diagnosis, lack of proper awareness, and the...

item.title: Pakistan-Iran Border Trade Persists Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: Despite the ongoing war in the Middle East, border trade between Pakistan and Iran has continued without major interruption. Key border crossings including Taftan, Gabad, Mand, and Panjgur remain open...

item.title: Explosion and Fire at Valero Refinery in Texas After Fluid Release
item.description: On March 23, 2026, a sudden fluid release at Valero's Port Arthur refinery triggered an explosion and fire, leading to a temporary shutdown of the 380,000-barrel-per-day oil refinery. Fortunately, the...

item.title: SC Rules on Preliminary Hearing in Contempt of Court Cases
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has reinforced the importance of a preliminary hearing in contempt of court matters. In a ruling delivered on March 25, 2026, the court emphasized that before framing cha...

item.title: Tanzania and South Africa Strengthen Collaboration to Promote Kiswahili in Schools
item.description: Tanzania and South Africa are increasing efforts to implement the 2022 Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) that focuses on boosting Kiswahili teaching in South African schools. This agreement was made t...

item.title: Iran conflict pushes up costs of medical equipment and supplies in India
item.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran has caused a notable surge in the cost of medical supplies in India. Manufacturers of surgical gowns, protective gear, syringes, dialysis kits, and dental implants ...

item.title: US Sends Peace Proposal as Iran Allows Safe Passage for Oil Vessels in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Donald Trump has proposed a peace plan to Iran as Tehran announced it will allow 'non-hostile' oil vessels through the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The move comes after weeks of heightened...

item.title: Senate inquiry highlights rise of climate misinformation and its impact on Australian democracy
item.description: Australia is experiencing a surge of misinformation and disinformation concerning climate change and energy, intensified by the use of artificial intelligence and the widespread reach of social media,...

item.title: Using cost-per-wear to encourage sustainable clothing choices
item.description: A recent study explores how informing consumers about the 'cost per wear' of clothing could influence purchasing behaviour and promote more sustainable fashion. Cost per wear is calculated by dividing...

item.title: Holy Week Travel: Ferry and Bus Fares Frozen, Says Marcos
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced that ferry and bus operators have committed not to increase fares during the upcoming Holy Week, aiming to ease the financial burden on Filipinos, e...

item.title: Dickson Calls for APC to Be Removed in 2027 Elections Over Poor Governance
item.description: Seriake Dickson, former governor of Bayelsa State, has called for the removal of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in the 2027 general elections. Speaking on Channels Television’s Politics Today, Di...

item.title: Thandeka Tshabalala Receives Support After Big Brother Mzansi Loss
item.description: Thandeka Tshabalala, the runner-up of Big Brother Mzansi's 2026 season, has garnered significant public support after losing out on the R2-million grand prize to Liema Pantsi. Fans of Thandeka, who be...

item.title: Professor Alex Eseimokumo Critiques Nigeria's Political Opposition and Calls for Better Elections
item.description: Professor Alex Eseimokumo, former Rivers State legislator, has raised concerns about the lack of a strong opposition in Nigeria, describing most political parties as ‘preposition parties.’ According t...

item.title: South Africa Tackles Tuberculosis Stigma and Treatment Hurdles on World TB Day
item.description: As South Africa observes World TB Day, a renewed focus is being placed on the ongoing challenges faced by those affected by tuberculosis. The disease remains a significant public health concern, hampe...

item.title: Trump Confirms Iran Sent Oil-Linked Gift Amid Ongoing Diplomatic Talks
item.description: For Tuesday, President Donald Trump yarn say Iran don send United States one kain special gift wey concern oil and gas, and e help push diplomatic talks wey fit end wahala. Trump no wan yan wetin the ...

item.title: Young Africans, Simba, and Azam Battle for Lead in Tanzania's Premier League
item.description: The Tanzania Mainland Premier League is currently experiencing an intense title race as defending champions Young Africans (Yanga) sit at the top of the table with 38 points from 16 matches. They are ...

item.title: Rising Costs and Tax Pressure Affect Gig Workers' Take-Home Pay
item.description: Gig workers in South Africa are increasingly taking on multiple jobs to keep up with the rising costs of living, including food, transport, and rent. While their gross income may increase, many find t...

item.title: CERN Successfully Tests Antimatter Transport System
item.description: CERN scientists have achieved a groundbreaking milestone by successfully transporting antimatter, specifically antiprotons, over a 10-kilometre stretch on their campus. This world-first test drive of ...

item.title: House Committee Turns Over Classified Records to Prosecutors in Brennan Investigation Ahead of Charging Decision
item.description: Federal prosecutors are set to receive classified documents from the House Intelligence Committee as part of the ongoing Justice Department investigation into former CIA Director John Brennan. The com...

item.title: Tezgam train derails near Bahawalpur, injuring 26 passengers
item.description: A serious train accident occurred on Tuesday night when seven coaches of the Karachi-bound Tezgam Express derailed near Adam Wahan Railway Station on the Bahawalpur–Lodhran section. The train, travell...
